title: MiFID II Conflicts of Interest — Gift and Entertainment Threshold Breach Trending

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

app: Splunk DB Connect to ethics register, or `inputlookup`
spl:
index=compliance sourcetype="ethics:gift" earliest=-365d
| eval gd=strptime(gift_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| bin gd span=90d
| stats sum(value_eur) as rolling_value by employee_id, gd
| where rolling_value>1000 AND approved_flag!="true"
| sort - rolling_value
value: Inducements create conflicts. Aggregating declared gifts per employee and counterparty against policy caps evidences proactive monitoring of inducement risk alongside gifts-and-entertainment registers.
implementation: (1) Set EUR cap per policy; (2) multi-currency conversion upstream; (3) route to COO office; (4) quarterly attestations; (5) map to inducements policy section.
visualization: Table (breaches), Column chart by quarter, Single value (employees over cap).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
